ExecutionPacket.purpose: Implement the approved module change without architectural drift.
ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt: Copy the relevant module contract from docs/development-plan.xml and any existing file-local MODULE_CONTRACT for files in write scope.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt: Copy the relevant graph entry from docs/knowledge-graph.xml.

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: Summarize only the public dependency contract required for the work.

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: Copy the relevant module verification entry from docs/verification-plan.xml.

assumptions.assumption-1: Dependency contracts and write scope are already approved by the controller.

stop-conditions.condition-1: Stop if a new dependency, new module, or unclear third-party surface is required.
stop-conditions.condition-2: Stop if generated files drift outside the approved write scope.
stop-conditions.condition-3: Stop if the requested behavior contradicts requirements or verification plan entries.
stop-conditions.condition-4: Stop if an explicit coverage-phase packet falls below 100 percent, or if any new allowlist entry lacks a replacement verification gate. Outside coverage phases, record coverage gaps as follow-up risk instead of expanding the task.
stop-conditions.condition-5: Stop if a new or meaningfully edited governed file outside `docs/*.xml` would remain without current MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, or required semantic anchors.

notes.note-1: Workers should only read more context when the packet is insufficient.
notes.note-2: The controller owns shared GRACE artifact updates unless the packet explicitly delegates them.
notes.note-3: Coverage workers must report exact coverage artifacts, any allowlist changes, and whether `bun run test:coverage` or a narrower target was run. Non-coverage workers should prefer the narrowest behavior-focused verification for the active development task.
notes.note-4: Workers must add or update file-local GRACE markup for governed files they create or meaningfully edit, then report that delta to the controller.

notes.note-1: Only list graph facts that really changed in the code.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Failure path must not perform the success side effect.

wave-follow-up.item-1: Run merged-surface checks for dependent modules.

phase-follow-up.item-1: Run the broader regression suite before marking the phase done.

observed-evidence.log-1: Observed log marker or missing marker.
observed-evidence.trace-1: Observed trace or command output summary.

FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Read the contract, inspect the divergent block, and apply the smallest safe fix.

evidence-captured.item-1: Focused test passed with expected marker or assertion.

CheckpointReport.next-action: Ready for controller review or shared-artifact sync.
